intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate based on visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or entirely subterranean. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not visible as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by a white rabbit anchor and soil debris.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only dark, damp soil. No plant matter.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains soil and a white rabbit figurine. No identifiable plant tissue.
plant_audit.P3: The Pothos is not visible; the pot is dominated by the rabbit anchor and soil.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no signs of surface cracking.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: A metallic spoon appears in the T-4 image, and a cylindrical object (possibly a sensor or tube) appears in T-3 through Current. These are external interventions.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Introduction of a spoon (T-4) and a cylindrical object (T-3).
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_current: The cylindrical object remains; no biological growth or decay observed as the plants were already absent/non-visible.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently devoid of visible plant life. The environment appears to be a dormant or failed propagation setup. The presence of moisture suggests the soil is being maintained, but the lack of vegetative response indicates a total failure of the specimens.
anomalies: Introduction of a cylindrical sensor/tube (T-3 to Current).
Absence of all registered plant life.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a stagnant botanical environment. The registered plants (P1-P4) are not visible in the provided image sequence. The pots contain only soil and the white rabbit anchor. The only changes observed are the temporary placement of a spoon and the persistent presence of a cylindrical object, likely a sensor. There is no evidence of active plant growth.
